title: StudioDash Features
description: StudioDash feature areas for revenue, app status, website analytics, provider health, trends, and reporting.
summary: StudioDash combines portfolio metrics, provider health, analytics summaries, and reporting tools into a native workspace for indie developers.

featureShowcases.heading: Feature workspace built around recurring developer questions
featureShowcases.summary: The feature set is organised around the checks developers repeat most often: revenue movement, app status, website visibility, provider health, and reporting.
featureShowcases.body: StudioDash is not presented as a decorative dashboard. The feature workflow is designed to help a developer move from a broad portfolio scan to the provider, app, revenue, or website detail that explains what changed.
bullets: Groups revenue, app, website, and provider signals into one operating model.
Supports quick scanning while keeping deeper feature pages available.
Uses clear setup and roadmap language where a provider is not yet live.

image.alt: StudioDash feature workspace showing product health and analytics summary cards
image.caption: Feature areas are designed around repeated operating checks for indie app developers.

sections.heading: Feature areas
paragraphs: StudioDash is organised around the practical questions developers ask while running apps and games: how revenue is moving, what app statuses need attention, whether provider data is healthy, and how website visibility is changing.
The product direction is intentionally operational rather than decorative. The dashboard is designed for repeated review, comparison, and action across a product portfolio.
bullets: Revenue summary cards for App Store, advertising, and website monetisation data where provider data is available.
App status views for App Store Connect metadata, lifecycle states, downloads, ratings, reviews, and release signals.
Website and SEO analytics using Cloudflare and Google Search Console coverage.
Integration health, sync status, and provider attention items.
Historical trends and selected-period summaries for comparing performance over time.

sections.heading: Native workspace
paragraphs: StudioDash is built as an Apple-platform app for Mac and iPad. The product is intended to provide a focused native workspace rather than another browser tab that competes with the provider dashboards it summarises.
The dashboard layout brings high-level cards, provider-specific views, and detailed tables together so developers can scan quickly and drill into the areas that need attention.

sections.heading: Roadmap context
paragraphs: Some feature areas are represented as roadmap or placeholder coverage while the product remains in development. Planned functionality is documented as planned, not as currently live.
Future work is expected to expand reporting, automation, and provider coverage while preserving the core goal: a useful, public-facing dashboard for developers running real products.
